design_philosophy.core_loop: Free-form flight exploration and aircraft operation across multiple US regions with optional combat mode
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastery of realistic flight mechanics versus accessibility for amateur pilots
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a pilot: operate a Cessna 182 Skylane with realistic controls and weather systems, or engage in WWI dogfighting as a fighter pilot
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to aeronautical realism over arcade accessibility; treated as a technical showcase for IBM PC compatibility rather than a game with fail states or traditional win conditions

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Open-ended sandbox design with optional combat mode; no documented difficulty settings or failure mechanics

progression.unlock_mechanism: Multiple geographic regions available (Chicago, Los Angeles, New York City, Seattle) plus alternative 'Europe 1917' combat mode
replayability_hooks: Variable weather and time of day systems
WWI dogfighting mode as alternative gameplay mode
Free-form exploration across four distinct US regions

world_building_techniques: Geographic authenticity (real airports and landmarks like Meigs Field, Lake Michigan, city skylines)
Alternative historical scenario (Europe 1917 mode with Sopwith Camel aircraft)

faction_indicators: Optional 'declare war' mechanic in Europe 1917 mode suggests adversarial engagement
danger_signals: Enemy aircraft encounters in WWI combat mode
environmental_storytelling: Real-world airport locations (Meigs Field as default, Chicago skyline, Lake Michigan)
Multiple US cities as distinct flight regions
Historical WWI setting in combat mode

sources_used: Wikipedia: Microsoft Flight Simulator (1982 video game)
